parsed_verdict.reasoning: The rule set contains 7 distinct top-level rules (same 7 in Stage 1 and Stage 2), well within the parsimony threshold. Each rule addresses a distinct phenomenon (natural directions, weight-speed, shape-speed, medium hindrance, floating, ground-effort, projectile motion) with no paraphrase pairs. All rules trace to specific observations (1-12) and use permitted Aristotelian elaborations (natural place, medium resistance, impressed/carried motion with explicit fading). Cross-rule references appear explicitly (e.g., 'restoring Rule 1', 'Rule 3 vs Rule 2', 'Rule 6 strictly couples...'), satisfying N12.
